channel.title: Oak on My Personal Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Oak on My Personal Blog

item.title: Steel and Oak Furniture
item.description:
Let’s change gears once again: furniture.

A series of furniture pieces made from steel and laminated oak.

Inspired by a collection from a well-known northern French retailer’s catalog from years ago 🤣.

Quality Materials

Except I made these with real materials:

  • 2mm tube in 40x40 and 45x45 for benches and table
  • 40x20 tube for the coffee table
  • Laminated oak tops in 22mm and 44mm for table and benches
  • Removable extension integrated under the table
  • Edges trimmed with router (my new best friend!)

Built to Last

Very heavy construction that will last. The “client” seems delighted 😁

item.title: Support Table for a LEGO DataCenter Model
item.description:
Certainly the largest / most beautiful of my creations. Months of gestation and sorting, disassembly, component cutting…

With the decommissioning of Cisco 6000 chassis, the reuse of old memory modules from an IBM Z, GPUs from a very old HPC and failed hard drives from another HPC. This table, given its dimensions, had to be made with a long-standing metal partner, the company Pizette.

It is in the lobby of the EDF Nanterre site, and serves as a support for a Lego model of a DC.

item.title: TV/Amplifier Cabinet - DELL HPC
item.description:
During the relocation of this HPC from one room to another… the first failed blades were set aside…. once the components were recovered, the blade chassis should have gone to the industrial waste bin… They were ultimately used to create a TV/Amplifier cabinet. This is the first in a series to use this concept of metal chassis with IT components and glass on top.

Possibility to make more.

Dimensions: WDH: 142 x 53 x 41 cm
